traffic_stats.title: Traffic statistics
traffic_stats.lead: Which parts of parlhub get requested, in which language, and by whom. Aggregated once a day from the server's own request log — no cookies, no tracking, no IP addresses. The counts are requests, not people.
traffic_stats.no_data: No figures yet. The first day's statistics appear once the daily aggregation has run.
traffic_stats.view_aria: Switch between daily and monthly figures
traffic_stats.route_other_note: “(other)” collects every request to a path that isn’t part of the site — usually automated scanners, malformed or malicious bot requests, or mistyped URLs. They are grouped into one row so a flood of random paths cannot inflate the statistics.
traffic_stats.chart_alt: Stacked bar chart of requests per period by {dim}: {periods} periods, {count} requests in total. The table below contains the same data.
traffic_stats.chart_alt_excl: Stacked bar chart of requests per period by {dim}: {periods} periods, {count} requests in total, excluding internal health checks. The table below contains the same data.
traffic_stats.table_caption: Requests by {dim} — the same data as the chart above.
traffic_stats.footnote: p95 is derived from latency buckets, so it is accurate to the bucket edge. Averages are weighted by request count.

metas.traffic_stats.title: Traffic statistics
metas.traffic_stats.desc: Which parts of parlhub get requested, in which language, and by whom — aggregated once a day from the server's own request log. No cookies, no tracking, no IP addresses.
